missables: Luck Sphere (Cloister Destruction Sphere) — must place Destruction Sphere before exiting the Cloister
Lv. 1 Key Sphere — rocky trail right side of chasm during temple escape
Lv. 2 Key Sphere — hidden behind Kimahri in regroup area beneath the ice
Avenger — hidden chest beside Auron in regroup area beneath the ice

subLocations.name: Lake Macalania
subLocations.prose: The Travel Agency is your last save point and shop before two back-to-back boss fights — stock up. Clasko is outside and gives a useful hint about chocobo breeding; suggest he try it and he'll reward you later with a Friend Sphere. Primer XVI is on the ground near him. Save before heading into the temple — you won't be able to come back.

items.name: 4000 Gil
items.notes: Chest near the Travel Agency — blocked until after Clasko moves; collect once events progress.

items.name: Mega-Potion
items.notes: Chest on the right side of the road near the temple entrance (crevasse, near north exit).

subLocations.name: Macalania Temple
subLocations.prose: Inside, Tromell gives a Shell Targe and Shelinda is here — collect both X-Potions and the 5,000 gil from the main hall before entering the Cloister. After the Seymour fight you're immediately on the run through the ice tunnel — Guado Guardians chase you out, and if one catches you it triggers a battle. You won't return to Macalania Temple. The Guado Guardians blocking the temple path fight in groups — have Rikku use Al Bhed Potions for party-wide healing and keep Auron or Tidus up front for physical damage; there's a brief cutscene confrontation with Seymour before you reach the Cloister.

items.name: 400 Gil
items.notes: Speak to Linna (Al Bhed) at the bottom of the stairs on the road to the temple entrance.

items.name: Shell Targe
items.notes: Given by Tromell inside the temple entrance.

items.name: Remedy ×2
items.notes: Chest at the back of the lower chamber (Nuns' Chamber) after the story message.

items.name: Hi-Potion ×2
items.notes: Speak to the shaken witnesses (Yellow Nun) on the right in the lower chamber.

items.name: X-Potion ×2
items.notes: Chest on the south side of the main hall.

items.name: 5000 Gil
items.notes: Chest on the north side of the main hall.

items.name: Elixir
items.notes: Speak to the Purple Monk in the northwest chamber (Monks' Chamber).

items.name: Ether
items.notes: Speak to the Brown Monk in the northwest chamber (Monks' Chamber).

items.name: Phoenix Down ×3
items.notes: Chest at the back of the northwest chamber (Monks' Chamber).

items.name: Luck Sphere
items.notes: Cloister of Trials — place the Destruction Sphere in the slot at the lowest level (step 17). Permanently missed if you exit the Cloister without doing this.

subLocations.name: Under the Ice
subLocations.prose: After Wendigo falls, the party sinks beneath the frozen lake. Use the Save Sphere to recover, then speak to each party member before Yuna leaves. Two chests are hidden in this area and easy to overlook — check behind Kimahri and to the left of Auron before moving on. A third chest is found earlier on the rocky trail during the escape from the temple.

items.name: Lv. 1 Key Sphere
items.notes: On the rocky trail on the right side of the chasm during the escape from the ice cavern. Cross to the left only after grabbing this.

items.name: Lv. 2 Key Sphere
items.notes: Partially hidden chest behind Kimahri in the regroup area beneath the ice.

items.name: Avenger
items.notes: Tidus's weapon with Counterattack — chest to the left of Auron in the regroup area beneath the ice.

bosses.strategy: The small Negator floating in the air prevents all spellcasting and summoning — take it out first. Focus Wakka on the Negator while the other fighters buff Wakka or chip the Crawler. Once the Negator is gone, magic and aeons become available again, but Crawler immediately starts counting down to its Mana Beam — a sweeping attack that can devastate the entire party. Summon an aeon and use its Shield ability to block the blast; aeons like Ifrit and Ixion can heal themselves by casting their own black magic while shielding. After blocking Mana Beam, pour Lightning spells and physical attacks into Crawler until it falls.

Two phases. Phase 1 (Seymour, 6,000 HP): Kill the Guado Guardians first — they carry Hi-Potions and use Auto-Potion to self-heal; steal their potions before attacking so they can't restore HP. Seymour opens with NulBlaze and NulFrost; use Dispel to strip them, then attack freely. Silence him to cut off magic. Phase 2: Anima appears (18,000 HP) after Seymour falls — switch to summoning your own Aeons or use Wakka/Lulu for ranged and magic damage. Have Yuna ready to heal throughout both phases.

Wendigo (18,000 HP) attacks immediately after Seymour with no save opportunity, so enter with full HP. It arrives flanked by two Guado Guardians who use Auto-Potion — steal their Hi-Potions before attacking or they will self-heal indefinitely. As a final act before dying, each Guardian casts protective spells and Berserk on the Wendigo; remove Berserk immediately with Auron's Threaten. With the Guardians gone, focus on Wendigo: use Lulu's spells for sustained damage — Wendigo has no confirmed elemental weakness, so any element works. Have Auron apply Power Break to reduce its heavy physical hits, Wakka can inflict Darkness or Sleep to limit its attacks, and Kimahri's Jinx lowers its luck. If your party is low on HP from the Seymour fight, use Rikku's Al Bhed Potions — they restore 1,000 HP to the whole party and bypass NulAll. Steal: Hi-Potion (common) / X-Potion (rare). Drop: Power Sphere.
